files.content: export async function charge(userId: string, amount: number) { /* stripe */ }

import { handleLogin } from './auth/login';
import { charge } from './billing/charge';

assertions.text: At invocation, the agent warns the user that /map-codebase is token-intensive by design
The agent asks the user for the starting scope before beginning the sweep
The agent offers subagent-parallel mapping as an option
At Phase 2, the agent presents 3+ fundamentally different candidate clusterings, each with a distinct named lens (not variations on one theme)
At Phase 3, after lens selection, the agent presents 2-3 slicing granularity options (coarse / medium / fine)
Eventually generates docs/arrows/ directory with index.yaml and per-segment arrow docs
Generates skeleton LLD files in docs/llds/ for each approved segment
Generates EARS spec files in docs/specs/ with reserved segment-name prefixes
Terminal response directs the user to continue with the linked-intent-dev workflow segment-by-segment
Ensures CLAUDE.md is configured (contains LID directives and a ## LID Mode: heading) before completing

files.content:
## LID Mode: Full

Use linked-intent-dev for all code changes.

assertions.text: The agent detects that partial LID docs exist and asks the user whether to treat the existing docs as authoritative or supersede them
The agent does NOT silently overwrite the existing HLD or the existing auth LLD

files.content:
## LID Mode: Full

LID is installed.

assertions.text: The agent recognizes the project as fully LID-configured (HLD, LLD, and EARS specs present) but missing the docs/arrows/ overlay
The agent redirects the user to /arrow-maintenance rather than running the brownfield sweep
No new LLDs, EARS specs, or arrow docs are generated in this run
